hypothesis: Systems-optimizer recovery and reporting become safer if terminal state pointers must resolve to the latest retained verdict of their status, not just any contract-aligned kept or rejected card.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` accepted `state.last_kept_change` and `state.last_rejected_change` as long as each pointer matched some kept or rejected backlog item, hypothesis card, and retained learning entry, so terminal reporting could still lag behind a newer retained verdict. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any terminal state pointer that does not resolve to the latest retained `learning_log.recorded_at` for its status while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails stale `last_kept_change` or `last_rejected_change` pointers and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Terminal systems-optimizer pointers must track the latest retained verdict per status; contract-aligned older pointers still make recovery and reporting stale.
